stimulus: According to engineer Henry Petroski, the best way to predict how long a non-living object (such as a bridge or a tool) will continue to function is to examine how long it has lasted so far. In this view, an object’s age is the strongest ______blank how much longer it will endure.
stem: Which choice completes the text with the most logical and precise word or phrase?
rationale: Choice D is correct because "predictor of" matches the idea that age is the best basis for predicting how much longer an object will endure. Choice A is incorrect because uncertainty contradicts a useful prediction. Choice B is incorrect because motivation doesn’t fit. Choice C is incorrect because criticism doesn’t describe prediction.
